name: The White House
subject: Fighting for Hardworking Americans’ Hard-Earned Wages
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=8e867dd09c25c4b0507b801f616854fa9447b04449858976ce65978bb07d72765a5f6c4301a7f529368cceadfb3db2094b259d1975bf8305

Lower Taxes, Higher Wages: A Tax Plan Made in America, for America

President Donald J. Trump’s tax reform initiatives will save Americans’ hard-earned dollars by dramatically cutting the Federal corporate income tax rate, which also promotes higher wages. Under the Unified Framework for Tax Reform, the “offshoring model” will no longer deposit American dollars overseas, instead using those dollars to bolster the American economy.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=8e867dd09c25c4b00e8da3afab5a6112557e496f8dd8fa0787c503c53bf52e71471604cc96f94cae31cdf08f305a4e63e130f0d3d6bcf9ee
Learn more about what the Trump Administration’s tax reform plan can do for you and your family.

Bipartisan Cooperation for Real Change in the American Tax Code
Yesterday, President Trump held a meeting at the White House with Senators from the bipartisan Senate Finance Committee. The group discussed tax cuts and reform and the President stressed the urgent need to deliver tax relief for the middle class by simplifying the burdensome tax code.
“The vast majority of Americans will be able to file their taxes on a single sheet of paper,” President Trump said. “Because not only will these be massive tax cuts, but these will be a big simplification.”
President Trump and the Administration look forward to future bipartisan cooperation in order to provide tangible results for the American people.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=8e867dd09c25c4b04a8e59a8d682492bc75fbf517f2953a43d53ed51e3032d1867b121a7c99e601f8d57fe4a3fc507e3f43c5e8b7b0be515
Watch President Trump's opening remarks.

Second Lady Karen Pence Announces Art Therapy Platform
Yesterday, Second Lady Karen Pence visited Florida State University in Tallahassee to announce her initiative titled Art Therapy: Healing with the HeART. Florida State University is home to a nationally acclaimed art therapy graduate education program. Following her announcement, Mrs. Pence visited Canopy Cove Eating Disorder Treatment Center to speak with therapists and clients about their experience with art therapy.
In an
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=8e867dd09c25c4b051bb9a6fc6e0bd065730668d7a9d4e269615243a0f6be12a4047cb81372661f2ec184663e6d23dcc702b7b0a61d18088
article published Wednesday, Mrs. Pence shared her story of learning about art therapy and selecting it for her platform.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=8e867dd09c25c4b034c36e1a8d97aa2852f07dfd1ff7416d80bae1f4a088fa1e73b4447e36a58fac9b22ca650da779e081cb247c23de5cd9
Watch the video to learn more about Second Lady Mrs. Pence's passion for art as an aid to healing and well-being.

Photo of the Day

President Trump participates in a Diwali Ceremonial Lighting of the Diya | October 17, 2017 (Official White House Photo by D. Myles Cullen)

POTUS TODAY

Today, the President and Vice President and senior White House officials will meet with Puerto Rico Governor Rosselló to discuss Puerto Rico’s recovery from two devastating hurricanes. This evening, President Trump and First Lady Melania Trump will attend a dinner at the Embassy of the State of Kuwait, where the First Lady will be honored by the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees.

COMING UP

Tomorrow, First Lady Melania Trump will present her Inauguration gown as the newest addition to the nearly 100-year old First Ladies Collection at the Smithsonian Museum of American History in Washington, D.C.

This weekend, the White House will be opening its gardens and grounds to visitors for the 2017 Fall Garden Tours. The grounds will be open on Saturday, October 21, from 10:00 AM to 4:30 PM, and Sunday, October 22, from 10:00 AM to 2:30 PM. This event is free and open to the public; however, a ticket is required for all attendees. The National Park Service will distribute free, timed tickets at a tent stationed near the entry point at the intersection of 15th Street and Constitution Avenue NW, each day beginning at 8:30 AM. More information can be found
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=8e867dd09c25c4b045a681973c2ebb8c6c9b9bf0722ad18e70a7c9b1ce852b3dbe695044a8058f1ea7c7b5c8519aee8ea1a0ebf100a2cfbd
here.

Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=8e867dd09c25c4b0a7c3788b99e9ebcea266f4e0e3ec31cb72e4ed26641dcb1c439438cb95a77ddca5e90524a89499cd5f917f2fd63ee553
Contact the White House |

* * *
| [ ](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2Farticles%2Fthank-you-for-fighting-for-a-faith-filled-future%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171019_enl_s1&n=3)

By Jason Yates At first glance, you might think this would be a quiet time of year at the ballot box. But nothing could be further from the truth. This fall there are over 2,000 elections with thousands of seats to be decided—from school boards, city councils, and governors, on through to the state legislature and Senate! | | [READ MORE ►](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2Farticles%2Fthank-you-for-fighting-for-a-faith-filled-future%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171019_enl_s1&n=4) |
---|---
* * *
[ ](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2Farticles%2Fmake-your-voice-heard-this-fall%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171019_enl_s1&n=5)
---
Even in this “off-election year,” there are thousands of elections taking place over the next few months—from local school boards, city councils, and mayors, to state legislature and Senate races. In fact, according to TurboVote.org, over 40% of the US population has an election in their area on November 7th. | | [LEARN MORE ►](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2Farticles%2Fmake-your-voice-heard-this-fall%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171019_enl_s1&n=6) |

_**Maddow Show: "Polls point to a competitive Senate race in Alabama"**_
Big news, team.

According to _The Rachel Maddow Show_ , recent polling shows that Democratic candidate Doug Jones is running a competitive campaign to take on right-wing extremist Roy Moore in the Alabama special election.

**But we're still a long way off from scoring an upset victory.** Doug must compete for each and every vote leading up to the election -- and our support right now will ensure he can get his message out across the state.

[**Chip in $1 now to help Doug Jones kick off his Senate campaign against Roy Moore with a strong show of support and help Democratic Senate candidates compete in every state.**](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/take-on-republicans-everywhere?refcode=DPE-20171019-NewPoll_DS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")

Check out what else _the Maddow Show_ had to say about the race:

> _"Moore is quite possibly the most radical major-party nominee in any statewide race Americans have seen in a generation ...[Doug Jones'] credibility as a candidate improves his chances of pulling off an upset."_
Roy Moore is a far-right ideologue who's too extreme for Alabama -- and his nomination gives us an edge in this race. But if we're going to succeed, we have to ensure Doug has the resources necessary to compete now through Election Day.

name: Democrats.org
subject: VIRGINIA: Volunteer to help Democrats win
body:
This election is an all-hands-on-deck moment for Virginia Democrats.
---
Election Day in Virginia is only 19 days away, Derek.

The work we'll do between now and when the last polls close will decide if Democrats win or lose key races all across the commonwealth. That's why our organizers and grassroots supporters are knocking on every door in every county.

**Will you join us?**

**[I'M IN](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Commit-to-Volunteer-in-Virginia?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+IM+IN&utm_campaign=em_20171019_dnc3_vav&source=em_20171019_dnc3_vav")**
Grassroots Democrats from all over Virginia are fired up to elect Ralph Northam, Justin Fairfax, Mark Herring, and the countless amazing Democrats running for Virginia House of Delegates -- leaders who will fight every day for our values as Democrats and Americans. Winning on November 7th will show Republicans that when they stand with Donald Trump, Virginia voters will hold them accountable.

[**Sign up so you can join us!**](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Commit-to-Volunteer-in-Virginia?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+Sign+up+so+you+can+join+us&utm_campaign=em_20171019_dnc3_vav&source=em_20171019_dnc3_vav")

The Democrats

This organization is powered by you, and we'd love to hear your ideas. name: Tim Kaine
subject: A dangerous path
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
Derek:

President Trump's decision to step back from the Iran nuclear deal is dangerous.

First, no one can deny that Iran has a track record of destructive behavior. That's why Congress recently passed a bipartisan sanctions bill giving the President more power to sanction behavior that destabilizes the region.

But the Iran nuclear deal is working. The IAEA -- the international agency monitoring the deal -- says Iran is in compliance. The Secretary of Defense says the deal is in our national interest. Our allies support the deal.

The President's decision to step back from a diplomatic deal that is working sends a troubling message about our priorities. Together with other actions by the President -- cutting the budget for the State Department, leaving key diplomatic posts unfilled, publicly undercutting diplomatic negotiations -- we see a clear pattern to weaken our efforts to peacefully negotiate. Those who destroy diplomacy raise the risk of unnecessary war.

If the US steps back from the Iran deal, Iran may well restart its nuclear weapons program. How is that in our interest?

And if the US walks away from a nuclear deal that's working, why would North Korea ever enter into a diplomatic deal about its own program?

Congress should stop the President's effort to break our diplomatic deal and make the world less safe. **[Will you add your voice and join me in urging Congress not to start down a path to walk away from the Iran nuclear agreement?](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=dNjAgqyRBcXr8INLbt0bCtFwNTfC2lja)**

Thanks,

Tim Kaine | | [**DONATE**](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=GSM9ci2UNU%2FNmpl638lNS9FwNTfC2lja)
---

body:
November 6th marks exactly one year until the midterm election -- and we're calling on grassroots activists to host house parties for a National Day of Action on November 5th. Sign-up to host >>
---
**[](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954dd7d/2b07114c/e1a32cf2/7316b3d8/4166814363/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/hold-a-house-party?source=PT-20171019-ActionAlertDS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")**
Team --

November 6th marks exactly one year until the midterm election -- and we're doing something extra special to prepare for the big day.

We're calling on grassroots activists to host house parties for a National Day of Action on November 6th. Gather your friends, family, and neighbors to discuss how to make sure that Election Day in 2018 is a day where we deliver a powerful message to Trump, Bannon, and McConnell.

We need to help all our Democratic Senators launch strong, tough campaigns -- and that means grassroots fundraising and organizing starts NOW.

**Team, will you sign up to host a house party in your community?**

> [**Yes, I 'm onboard to host a house party to help our Senate Democrats with grassroots organizing.**](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954dd7d/2b07114c/e1a32cf2/7316b3d8/4166814363/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/hold-a-house-party?source=PT-20171019-ActionAlertDS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")
>
> [**No, I can 't host, but I'd like to contribute to help support Senate Democrats up for re-election next year to give them the resources they need to launch strong, tough campaigns.**](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/support-democrats?refcode=DPE-20171019-ActionAlertDS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")
If this is your first time hosting, don't worry, we'll send you a house party kit to make sure your party is a success.

From fighting for Obamacare to protecting our DREAMers and safeguarding our planet, Senate Democrats are doing all that it takes to fight for every American -- not just those at the top.

That's why organizing to reach your friends and family is so critical right now. We need to ensure that our Senate Democrats have the resources they need to defeat their Republican challengers in 2018.

And the best way to make sure your friends and family know what's at stake?**Invite them to be a part of our Day of Action!**

Thanks for helping us get ready for 2018,

Team Grassroots

**Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee
120 Maryland Ave. NE
Washington, DC 20002** Paid for by the DSCC, dscc.org,
and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
The DSCC is the only committee solely dedicated to supporting Democrats in the U.S. Senate. This message was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com because you are a part of the DSCC's grassroots network. Please add us to your address book or approved senders list to make sure you keep receiving DSCC updates. Emails are a critical way for us to stay in touch with our grassroots supporters. 99% of the DSCC's contributions come from grassroots donors giving less than $200. We also strive to keep you updated on issues related to the Democratic Party's values.

If you'd like to take a break from DSCC emails, [click here](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954dd7d/2b07114c/e1a32cf2/7316b3da/4166814363/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/take-an-email-break?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com").

If you'd like to make a contribution to support us, please [click here](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954dd7d/2b07114c/e1a32cf2/7316b3d5/4166814363/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/supportdscc?refcode=DPE-disclaimer"). Together, we're going to take back the Senate, but we'll need the help of every single grassroots supporter to do it. If you'd like to stop receiving all emails from the DSCC, please [click here](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954dd7d/2b07114c/e1a32cf2/7316b3d4/4166814363/VEsA/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/content/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com"). We greatly appreciate your continued support. Contributions or gifts to the DSCC are not tax deductible.
